narrative: All three coins bounced sharply — ethereum leading at +4.05% to $1,930 — as markets found footing despite lingering U.S.-Iran tensions and AI-stock overhang from China's Kimi model, which dragged crypto lower mid-week per CoinDesk. Prices and news diverge on the Clarity Act: Polymarket bettors cut passage odds to a record low as Senate Democratic opposition holds and the ethics-section deadlock drags on per CoinDesk, even as a White House high-level meeting is planned to force a resolution. The payment infrastructure race is accelerating independently of legislation — Stripe and SWIFT are racing for next-gen settlement rails while Visa's Open USD challenges Circle, and 84% of financial firms now call tokenization a strategic priority per a Broadridge survey cited by CoinDesk.
worth_watching: Tether USDT faces a 2-year countdown to compliance under the GENIUS Act per CoinDesk — rules remain unwritten a year in, with USDT's U.S. platform position at risk by 2028; a structural stablecoin market shift absent from watchlist coin metrics
Deribit $2.5B BTC call spreads targeting $72K at month-end when the Fed meets per CoinDesk — an options market signal pointing 10%+ above current price, not visible in spot watchlist prices

Kraken launched simplified USD-settled BTC and ETH options per CoinDesk, arguing product complexity (not demand) held back adoption — a derivatives access story that could expand the options market beyond watchlist spot metrics

headlines: Clarity Act negotiations: High-level White House meeting planned, sources say
U.S. Senate unanimously opposes clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried
The Clarity Act isn't a ticket to sanctions evasion, actually
The privacy paradox of protecting kids online
The Clarity Act is the most important consumer protection effort in years
AI is destroying the internet. Math is our only hope.
The payment war shifts to distribution as stablecoins reach mainstream status
Polymarket traders cut Clarity Act passage odds to record low as Senate delay drags on
